one_line_summary: Well-read, well-structured docs set with strong architectural coverage, but the tutorial contains some non-runnable or misleading steps and the API reference overstates signature fidelity relative to the harness results.
strengths: Architecture doc is organized, concrete, and covers flow-of-control, extension points, security layers, concurrency, and non-goals with useful specificity.
Session log clearly shows substantial source reading and live verification of imports and several tutorial snippets before finalizing the docs.
Index and cross-links make the set navigable, and the prose is generally clear, technical, and professional.
API reference has broad module coverage across duh/kernel and duh/ports, meeting the coverage goal substantially.
weaknesses: Consistency harness shows only 42.6% signature match and 25% import-check success, so the claim that signatures were copied verbatim is not fully borne out.

Tutorial is weakened by inaccurate or non-runnable guidance such as creating files outside docs-new, using placeholder expected outputs like XX/Y.Ys, and an unresolved CLI help snippet mismatch at the end of the session.
Cross-artefact coherence is only moderate because some architecture/tutorial claims are more confident than the API faithfulness results justify.
API examples and behavioral descriptions sometimes go beyond what the harness could validate, increasing risk of drift or invented details.
